title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective validated ServeMux route and precedence table; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in validated ServeMux route and precedence table and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Go 1.26 ServeMux patterns with optional method, host, literal segments, single wildcards, remainder wildcards, and end markers.
Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Route Registration Gate
steps.instruction:
Order the Route Registration Gate Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Route Registration Gate.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Route Registration Gate assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Route Registration Gate, keep Route Registration Gate connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A debug team handles case 2149 in a recovery rehearsal with an ambiguous database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose a specific method-and-path route and preserve one registration owner; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Route Registration Gate has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Route Registration Gate, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Route Registration Gate precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.

For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Route Registration Gate, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: If every intended path has a handler, the route table is complete and non-conflicting.

options.text: Declare Go server method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the debug Route Registration Gate case
Predict Go server resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the debug Route Registration Gate case
Run the smallest deterministic Go server request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the debug Route Registration Gate case
Inject one changed Go server failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the debug Route Registration Gate case
